claims.text: Each integration is verified by an automated build and an automated test suite to detect integration errors as quickly as possible.

evidence_spans.text: Each integration is verified by an automated build and an automated test suite to detect integration errors as quickly as possible.

claims.text: Code coverage measures the percentage of lines exercised by the test suite.

evidence_spans.text: Code coverage measures the percentage of lines exercised by the test suite.

claims.text: An incident is an unplanned interruption or degradation of a service.

evidence_spans.text: An incident is an unplanned interruption or degradation of a service.

claims.text: The solo method is designed for a single developer working alone. It is appropriate for prototypes, personal projects, and small tools.

evidence_spans.text: The solo method is the lightest of the four SDLC methods and is designed for a single developer working alone.
The solo method is appropriate for prototypes, personal projects, and small tools.

claims.text: The assured method is designed for regulated industries and for complex agentic systems at scale with ten or more bounded contexts.

evidence_spans.text: The assured method is designed for regulated industries and for complex agentic systems at scale with ten or more bounded contexts.

claims.text: A rolling deployment gradually replaces instances of the old version with the new version a few at a time. Rolling deployments avoid downtime but make rollback slower than a blue-green switch.

evidence_spans.text: A rolling deployment gradually replaces instances of the old version with the new version a few at a time. Rolling deployments avoid downtime but make rollback slower than a blue-green switch.

claims.text: A semantic version number takes the form major dot minor dot patch (three parts). The major version increases for incompatible changes, the minor version for backward-compatible features, and the patch version for backward-compatible bug fixes.

evidence_spans.text: Semantic versioning assigns a three-part version number of the form major dot minor dot patch. The major version increases for incompatible changes, the minor version for backward-compatible features, and the patch version for backward-compatible bug fixes.

claims.text: The page code-review.md explains pull request sizing for reviews, noting that small pull requests are reviewed more thoroughly than large ones, and keeping a change under about four hundred lines helps reviewers stay focused and find more defects.

evidence_spans.text: Small pull requests are reviewed more thoroughly than large ones. Keeping a change under about four hundred lines helps reviewers stay focused and find more defects.

claims.text: The page feature-flags.md covers turning features on and off at runtime, describing feature flags as configuration switches that let a team turn a feature on or off at runtime without deploying new code.

evidence_spans.text: A feature flag is a configuration switch that lets a team turn a feature on or off at runtime without deploying new code.
